sense: phrases that pattern in Q1–2 texts
def: common opener in announcements; stress ‘pleased’ and ‘announce’
example: We are pleased to announce the opening of our new branch.

sense: phrases that pattern in Q1–2 texts
def: advertising imperative; falling, emphatic tone
example: Don’t miss our biggest sale of the year!

sense: phrases that pattern in Q1–2 texts
def: introduces an address; read the number clearly
example: The store is conveniently located on Fifth Avenue.

sense: phrases that pattern in Q1–2 texts
def: closing call-to-action; steady pace to the end
example: For more information, visit our website or call us today.

sense: phrases that pattern in Q1–2 texts
def: a list/scale; rise on the first item, fall on the last
example: We offer classes ranging from beginner to advanced.

sense: phrases that pattern in Q1–2 texts
def: paired structure; keep parallel intonation
example: Whether you are a student or a professional, we have a plan for you.

word: This picture was taken in / at
def: your reliable opening line; sets the scene
example: This picture was taken in a modern office.

word: It appears to be / It looks like
def: make a safe guess about the situation
example: It looks like a team meeting is taking place.

word: The first thing I notice is
def: lead into the main subject naturally
example: The first thing I notice is a woman giving a presentation.

def: position elements front-to-back
example: In the foreground, a man is typing on a laptop.

word: on the left / right / in the centre
def: position elements left-to-right
example: On the right, there are several bookshelves.

word: they seem to be / they appear to be
def: guess at actions or feelings to add detail
example: They seem to be enjoying the discussion.

def: present continuous for actions in progress
example: A waiter is serving food to the customers.

def: state what exists in the scene
example: There are a few people standing near the door.

def: describe clothing precisely
example: The man is wearing a dark suit and glasses.

def: describe what someone has in hand
example: She is holding a cup of coffee.

word: next to / behind / in front of
def: prepositions linking objects in space
example: There is a plant next to the window.

word: is filled with / is full of
def: describe a crowded or busy scene
example: The room is filled with people.

def: describe a habit naturally
example: I usually go shopping on weekends.

def: state a preference clearly
example: Personally, I prefer online shopping to going to the mall.

def: add a reason to fill the time
example: I take the bus, mainly because it’s cheaper.

def: back a point with an (invented) example
example: For example, last week I bought a jacket online.

def: commit when asked to pick
example: If I had to choose, I’d say the afternoon.

def: soften or emphasise a quick personal answer
example: To be honest, I don’t do it very often.

def: frame an answer drawn from the table
example: According to the schedule, the session starts at 10 a.m.

word: is scheduled for / is set to begin at
def: state a time accurately
example: The keynote is scheduled for 9:30 in the main hall.

word: will be held in / will take place in
def: state a location from the table
example: The workshop will be held in Room 204.

def: confirm a true assumption before adding detail
example: Yes, that’s correct — lunch is served at noon.

word: Actually, / I’m afraid that’s not right
def: politely correct a wrong assumption
example: Actually, the meeting is on Tuesday, not Monday.

def: summarise a count from the table
example: There are three sessions before the break.

def: state your position plainly
example: I believe that working from home is more productive.

def: alternative opener for your view
example: In my opinion, students should learn a second language early.

def: signal the structure of your answer
example: I support this idea for two main reasons.

def: express a firm positive stance
example: I’m strongly in favour of flexible working hours.

def: acknowledge the other side, then commit
example: While some people think it’s risky, I believe it’s worth it.

def: casual, confident way to give a view
example: If you ask me, public transport is the better option.

def: introduce your first reason
example: First of all, it saves a lot of time.

def: add a second reason
example: Secondly, it reduces stress from commuting.

def: support a reason with a concrete case
example: For example, I can start work without a long commute.

def: draw a consequence from a point
example: This means that I have more time for my family.

def: back-pocket phrase to add time
example: Another point worth mentioning is the cost savings.

def: signal your conclusion
example: For these reasons, I strongly believe it’s the better choice.

def: a natural beat before a no-prep answer
example: Well, let me see… I’d say twice a week.

def: buys a moment on the opinion task
example: That’s a good question. Personally, I think…

def: links back and fills time coherently
example: As I mentioned, convenience is the main factor.

def: rephrase to recover and keep talking
example: What I mean is, it’s simply more efficient.

def: restate a point to extend an answer
example: In other words, it benefits both sides.

def: wrap up smoothly if time is short
example: All in all, it’s the option I’d recommend.
